svg.title: Removing the Special Meanings of Wild Cards

section.p: Wild cards are special characters used in the `ls` command to specify files or directories.

ul.li: [seq] - matches any character in the specified sequence
[!seq] - matches any character not in the specified sequence

section.data-narration: Removing the Special Meanings of Wild Cards
section.h2: Removing the Special Meanings of Wild Cards
section.p: To remove the special meanings of wild cards, you can use the `-n` or `--number` option of the `ls` command.
In this example, `*` is treated as a literal character and does not match any sequence of characters.
In this example, `*` is treated as a literal character and does not match any sequence of characters.
